extra.message: User input from the 'name' query parameter is embedded into a string via f-string (line 38) which is then passed to jinja2.Template(content).render(). This allows Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI). An attacker can inject Jinja2 template directives such as {{7*7}} or {{config.__class__.__init__.__globals__['os'].popen('id').read()}} to achieve remote code execution on the server.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')

extra.message:
User input from the 'name' query parameter is directly interpolated into HTML via f-string at line 38 (f"

Hello, {name}!

...") without any sanitization or escaping. An attacker can inject arbitrary JavaScript (e.g., ) which will be rendered in the victim's browser. Even though the string is passed through Jinja2 Template.render(), Jinja2 does not auto-escape by default when using Template() directly.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: The 'public_ip' value obtained from an external HTTP service response (line 36: public_ip_response.text) is directly interpolated into HTML via f-string at line 38 ({public_ip}) without sanitization. If the external IP service is compromised or returns malicious HTML/JavaScript, it will be rendered in the user's browser, enabling cross-site scripting.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: SUPER_SECRET_NAME is hardcoded as the string literal "John Ripper" in source code instead of being loaded from an environment variable or secret store. Anyone with access to the source code obtains this credential. The FIXME comment indicates this is a known issue that should use os.getenv().

SUPER_SECRET_TOKEN is hardcoded as the string literal "5u93R53Cr3tT0k3n" in source code instead of being loaded from an environment variable or secret store. Anyone with access to the source code obtains this sensitive token. The FIXME comment indicates this is a known issue that should use os.getenv().

FastAPI is initialized with debug=config.DEBUG (line 14), which is controlled by the DEBUG environment variable (config.py line 9). When enabled, FastAPI's debug mode exposes detailed error traces including source code and local variables in error responses. The .env_temp template file ships with DEBUG=True, risking information exposure in production if used directly.

cwe: CWE-200: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor
